raw.population: Adults N: 99 (Russian) Age: 27.4±11.9 Children N: 90 (Russian) Age: 9.6±6.8
raw.objective: “To estimate individual and population postinduction pharmacokinetics of carbamazepine (CBZ) in epileptic adult and paediatric patients who received chronic CBZ monotherapy.”
raw.covariates: Creatinine clearance on CL. WT on Vd. Different CL for adults and children.
raw.observations: No relationship between dose or serum concentrations and clearance. Renal excretion only constitutes 1-3% of clearance and thus may not be clinically relevant to include in model. The approach seems to provide good results, although difficult to implement for subsequent readers.
raw.equations: Adults: Ka= theta1 Kslope= 0.000045 [unit: (h*mL/min/1.73m^2)^-1] Ki=theta2 Vd=theta3*WT Children: Ka=4 Kslope=0.00005 Ki=theta5 Vd=theta6*WT Ke=Ki+Kslope*CCr CCr=creatinine clearance corrected for size (BSA)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.77 theta2=0.07 theta3=1.03 theta4=0.75 theta5=0.086 theta6=1.15 omega1=0.11 omega2=0.25 omega3=0.25 omega4=0.11 omega5=0.18 omega6=0.073

study.population: Adults N: 99 (Russian) Age: 27.4±11.9 Children N: 90 (Russian) Age: 9.6±6.8
study.objective: “To estimate individual and population postinduction pharmacokinetics of carbamazepine (CBZ) in epileptic adult and paediatric patients who received chronic CBZ monotherapy.”

covariates.raw: Creatinine clearance on CL. WT on Vd. Different CL for adults and children.

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
